title: Garter-stitch scarf
gaugeText: 18 sts × 28 rows = 10 × 10 cm in garter stitch on 5 mm needles, blocked.
sources: Weldon's Practical Knitting, 7th series (1880s), §Plain Scarf — the garter-stitch scarf is the most-reproduced beginner pattern in the Victorian knitting literature
notes: The canonical beginner project. Casts on long-tail, works garter (knit every row), casts off in pattern, weaves in ends. Mentions blocking lightly but doesn't require it for a wool scarf. No chart needed. Variations: chunky for a quick gift, DK for a longer evening project, cotton for a summer scarf.
